scenarios.description: children() returns empty Elements when no child nodes (childNodeSize()==0 branch)
scenarios.precondition: Element el = new Element("div"); // no children

scenarios.description: children() filters non-Element child (only one TextNode yields empty list)
scenarios.precondition: Element el = new Element("p"); el.appendChild(new TextNode("text"));

scenarios.description: children() returns only Element children filtering out non-Element nodes (mixed nodes)
scenarios.precondition: Element el = new Element("div"); el.appendChild(new TextNode("t")); el.appendChild(new Element("span")); el.appendChild(new Comment("c"));
scenarios.expectedResult: returned Elements list has one element with tagName() == "span"

scenarios.description: children() uses cached shadowChildrenRef on second call (cache hit branch)
scenarios.precondition: Element el = new Element("div"); el.appendChild(new Element("a")); el.appendChild(new Element("b")); // first call builds cache
scenarios.expectedResult: first call returns two children ["a","b"]; second call returns same list instance (cache used)
pseudocode: Elements first = el.children(); Elements second = el.children();

scenarios.description: children() returns independent Elements instance each call but same content when cache cleared by mutation
scenarios.precondition: Element el = new Element("div"); Element c1 = new Element("x"); el.appendChild(c1); List<Element> list1 = el.children(); el.appendChild(new Element("y")); // invalidates cache
scenarios.expectedResult: after mutation, children() returns two distinct elements ["x","y"], list1 remains size 1
pseudocode: assert list1.size() == 1; assert list2.size() == 2; assert list2.get(1).tagName().equals("y");
